Off Calendar Jan-31-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch appeared by telephone for and with Trustee Debra Dolch (present in Court).

The Court noted that it has a waiver of notice from an authorized representative of Archbishop Riordan High School and of St. Finn Barr Catholic Church, and questions why Counsel didn't talk to someone directly at those entities. Ms. Rasch stated that Ms. Carney is Counsel for the Archdiocese, that everything goes through the Archdiocese and that they started their talks with the person who handles the donations for those entities. Ms. Rasch also stated that both Parties told them that they had to go through the Archdiocese and that this is their process. In addition, Ms. Rasch stated that the petition for fees for the Guardian Ad Litem is coming up and that there is enough money in the reserve to pay for that. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Final Distribution of Trust, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Continued To Jan-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Continued To Jan-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).

The Court stated that there is an issue with lack of notice to Riordan and St. Finn Barr's. Ms. Rasch stated that those gifts were made more than more than 16-years ago by the prior Trustee and before Ms. Dolch was involved, that they received notice of irrevocability and copies of all of the trusts and all of the amendments, that they had 120-days to contest and that they are responsible to come forward. The Court stated that at that point there was no petition for final distribution and that it wants to see notice to Riordan and St. Finn Barr's and it wants to give them the opportunity to respond if they want to. Ms. Rasch requested an opportunity to ask Riordan and St. Finn Barr's to waive notice. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution of Trust, etc. is continued to January 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Rasch that if they don't waive notice, her petition will not be heard on that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Trausch. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder Guardian Ad Litem for Elizabeth Ryan (Beneficiary)
Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee)

Ms. Rasch explained that in 2014 a request for authority to provide case management services was granted at 8 hours per month until the next status hearing, there were a number of issues pending, that a report was filed, but no modification was made to the order. Ms. Rasch also stated that her client has continued the case management services as they are necessary, spoke of family dynamics, issues of lack of communication with the son and the son attempting to move the mother to another facility. The Court granted The 7th Account of Trustee, etc., authorizing continuation of case management services (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). Ms. Rasch is to provide said order.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Make Periodic Advances Against Distributive Share Of Remainder Beneficiary Michael Ryan
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with (petitioner) Debra Dolch. Bruce Feder as Guardian ad Litem for Elizabeth Ryan.

Ms. Rasch stated that there are no objections to the proposal, that she would like to add to the order that distribution is not mandatory and is conditioned upon the trustee determining that there are enough funds to provide for Mrs. Ryan's care prior to making periodic advances against Michael's share, that distribution is also conditioned upon Michael providing copies of the Bank of America statements, closing out that account, and distributing the balance of the account to Ms. Dolch. Mr. Feder stated that he wants to make sure that Mr. Ryan's 1/3 share is going to be sufficient to cover payment of the tuition and that the trustee do annual reviews to ensure that Mrs. Ryan's care is provided for and that said payments to not exceed Mr. Ryan's share. The Court granted the petition for authority to make periodic advances, etc. (subject to receipt/review of the amended order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Report Regarding One To One Care, Placement And A Report From The Case Manager
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Regarding Placement, Case Management Services, Social Security And Pension
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee). Bruce Feder Guardian Ad Litem for Elizabeth Ryan. Michael Ryan also present.

Discussion was held with regard to fees, sale of the Bank of America stocks, care costs/living costs going up due to the need to have a care attendant and the Conservatee being placed in an assisted living facility, the social security and pension benefits being brought into the trust, whether the Conservatee has capacity to execute a new health care directive to give Ms. Dolch authority regarding placement, the care facility always having to contact Ms. Dolch because they are unable to contact Michael, having a case manager who could advise the Trustee as to what services may be available, the Conservatee not wanting to move and liking the Mercy facility, the Conservatee having an understanding of who people are and who she wants in charge, the Conservatee's desire to help with the Granddaughters tuition (no idea as to the amount), other family members being in support of payment of said tuition, the issue of an offset on Michael's inheritance, the possibility of a $13,000.00 gift to the Girls to help with their tuition, etc. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss the matter. Upon returning, the Parties indicated that they have resolved the issues. Debra Dolch will take over receipt of the non trust income. There will be some discussion with Ms. Ryan regarding the Mercy facility, the need to move to another floor and the need for one to one care. The accounting petition and the petition for instructions were taken under submission. Filing of a status report evaluating one to one care, placement and a report from the case manager was set for June 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court authorized up to 8 hours a month for the case manager subject to modification. The issue of gifting is to be done by way of a noticed petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

The Court stated that the Examiner's notes were not complied with. Mr. McGovern stated that he did not receive the Examiner's notes. The Petition For Change Of Venue was denied. Ms. Rasch will prepare the order.

Petition For Authority To Make Gifts
Off Calendar Dec-20-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Trustee). John Cullen for Ms. Ryan.

Ms. Rasch reported that only the family members were receiving gifts at this time, and that gifts were requested for all 12 grandchildren. Mr. Cullen stated that Ms. Ryan was well aware of her assets, that there was no mental incapacity, and that the gifts were appropriate. Ms. Rasch and Mr. Cullen are in agreement of setting up custodial accounts for the grandchildren with the parents named as successor custodians. Notices to the other beneficiaries were waived. Ms. Rasch will prepare and submit the order after Mr. Cullens provides the name of the bank. The matter was set for status on the receipts on January 23, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.
